mission.title: clickhouse: [RFC] Table Engine with Unique Key support
mission.description: [RFC] Table Engine with Unique Key support. Requested by 19+ users.

steps.title: Check current clickhouse setup
steps.description:
Verify your clickhouse version and configuration:
```bash
clickhouse-client --version
```
This feature requires a working clickhouse installation.

steps.title: Review clickhouse configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant clickhouse configuration:
## Motivation
Real-time updates have a large number of application scenarios: like order scene, row level deduplication etc. 

Currently, in ClickHouse, we can use `ReplacingMergeTree` to meet this request somehow.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFC] Table Engine with Unique Key support
steps.description:
### Changelog category (leave one):
- New Feature

### Changelog entry (a user-readable short description of the changes that goes to CHANGELOG.md):
Add new table engine UniqueMergeTree.  Closes #41817.

Syntax:
```sql
CREATE TABLE [IF NOT EXISTS] [db.]table_name [ON CLUSTER cluster]
(
    name1 [type1] [DEFAULT|MATERIALIZED|ALIAS expr1],
    name2 [type2] [DEFAULT|MATERIALIZED|ALIAS expr2],
    ...
) ENGINE = UniqueMergeTree([ver])
[PARTITION BY expr]
[ORDER BY expr]
[PRIMARY KEY expr]
[UNIQUE

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "[RFC] Table Engine with Unique Key support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This RFC proposed a new UniqueMergeTree table engine using mark-delete + insert for real-time upsert semantics with better read performance than ReplacingMergeTree. An implementation was submitted in PR #44534 but was closed without being merged. The feature remains an unmerged proposal. Users needing unique key deduplication should continue using ReplacingMergeTree with FINAL or the newer lightweight DELETE functionality.

prerequisites.description: A working clickhouse installation or development environment.
